questions.question: Explain the key difference between heuristic and probabilistic approaches to link prediction.
expectedPoints: Heuristic methods use similarity scores based on network features
Probabilistic methods use statistical inference and probability theory
Probabilistic approaches provide calibrated probability estimates
Heuristic approaches typically only provide relative scores

questions.question: Describe the Stochastic Block Model and explain how it can be used for link prediction, including its assumptions and limitations.
expectedPoints: Assumes nodes belong to discrete latent communities
Connection probability depends only on group memberships
Uses Ω matrix to represent between-group connection probabilities
Works well for networks with clear community structure
Limitation: may oversimplify when communities aren't discrete
Parameter estimation typically done through maximum likelihood
Can be extended to mixed-membership models for flexibility
